title: Bridge Essay: Hierarchical Active Inference: A Theory of Motivated Control.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dependent-arising** (Phase 6) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Dependent-arising to mechanistic neuroscience.

**Hierarchical Active Inference: A Theory of Motivated Control.** — Trends Cogn Sci. PMID: 29475638. DOI: 10.1016/j.tics.2018.01.009. Motivated control refers to the coordination of behaviour to achieve affectively valenced outcomes or goals. The study of motivated control traditionally assumes adistinction between control and motivational processes, which map to distinct (dorsolateral versus ventromedial) brain systems. However, the respective roles and interactions between these processes remain controversial.
